title: Methods for assessing the severity of, and for inhibiting, pro-inflammatory responses with total cell-free dna
abstract: This invention relates to methods and compositions for assessing an amount of total cell-free DNA, such as from a subject having a pro-inflammatory response, such as a pro- inflammatory response associated with an infection, such as a COVID-19 infection. This invention also relates to compositions and methods for treating subjects having or suspected of having a pro-inflammatory response, such as a pro-inflammatory response associated with an infection, such as a COVID-19 infection. In embodiments, there is a risk of an inappropriate or detrimental pro-inflammatory response, such as in a subject with a COVID- 19 infection. The methods and composition provided herein can be used to determine severity and risk of complications in a subject. The methods and composition provided herein can also be used to assess the effectiveness of a therapy in a subject, such as a COVID-19 subject.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: CLAIMS 1. A method of assessing a sample from a subject having or suspected of having condition, such as an infection, associated with a pro-inflammatory response (e.g., a COVID- 19 subject), the method comprising: (a) determining an amount of total cf-DNA in a sample from the subject; and (b) optionally, reporting and/or recording the amount of total cf-DNA. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the subject has, is suspected of having, has had, or is at risk of having one or more complications. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the subject has, is suspected of having, has had, or is at risk of having pneumonia or acute respiratory distress syndrome. 4. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject is thought by a clinician to have a severe case of the infection. 5. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject is thought by a clinician to have a significantly increased risk of death. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the increase is relative to a healthy subject without the condition. 7. The method of claim 5, wherein the increase is relative to a subject with a mild case of the pro-inflammatory response or condition. 8. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the method further comprises: (c) comparing the amount of total cf-DNA to a threshold total cf-DNA value or at least one prior total cf-DNA amount. 9. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the method further comprises: (d) determining that the subject has, or as being at increased risk of having one or more complications of the condition or of death based on the determined amount of total cf-DNA compared to the threshold total cf-DNA value and/or at least one prior total cf-DNA amount. 10. A method of assessing a subject having or suspected of having condition associated with a pro-inflammatory response (e.g., a COVID-19 subject), the method comprising: (a) obtaining an amount of total cf-DNA in a sample from the subject, wherein the subject has, is suspected of having, has had, or is at risk of having one or more complications of the condition or of death; (b) comparing the amount of total cf-DNA to a threshold total cf-DNA value and/or at least one prior total cf-DNA amount; and (c) optionally, determining a treatment or monitoring regimen for the subject based on the determined amount of total cf-DNA compared to the threshold total cf-DNA value and/or at least one prior total cf-DNA amount. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the method further comprises classifying the subject as having or as being at increased risk of having one or more complications of the condition or of death based on the determined amount of total cf-DNA compared to the threshold total cf-DNA value and/or at least one prior total cf-DNA amount. 12. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject is on or is in need of mechanical support. 13. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein one or more additional test(s) prior applied to the subject was performed or the method further comprises performing one or more additional test(s) on the subject. 14. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the total cf-DNA amount is provided in a report. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the amount is provided in a report that also contains at least one prior amount of total cf-DNA that was in a sample from the subject. 16. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the total cf-DNA amount is recorded in a database. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the database also contains at least one prior amount of total cf-DNA that was in a sample from the subject. 18. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the amount of total cf-DNA is determined or obtained using an amplification-based quantification assay. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the amplification-based quantification assay is quantitative real-time PCR (qRT-PCR) or digital PCR. 20. The method of any one of claim 1-17, wherein the amount of total cf-DNA is determined or obtained using sequencing, such as high throughput or next generation sequencing. 21. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein an amount of total cf-DNA that is greater than the threshold value and/or is increased relative to the amount from an earlier time point represents an increased or increasing risk. 22. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein an amount of total cf-DNA that is lower than the threshold value and/or is decreased relative to the amount from an earlier time point represents a decreased or decreasing risk. 23. The method of any one of claims 10-22, wherein the determining a monitoring regimen comprises determining the amount of total cf-DNA in the subject over time or at a subsequent point in time, or suggesting such monitoring to the subject. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein the subject is assessed daily. 25. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the time between samples is decreased if the amount of total cf-DNA is increased relative to the threshold or an amount from an earlier time point. 26. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the additional amount(s) of total cf-DNA are recorded or reported. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the additional amount(s) are provided in a report. 28. The method of claim 26, wherein the additional amount(s) are recorded in a database. 29. The method of any one of claims 10-28, wherein the determining a monitoring regimen comprises using or suggesting the use of one or more additional test(s) to assess the subject. 30. The method of any one of claims 10-29, wherein the determining a treatment regimen comprises selecting or suggesting a treatment for the subject. 31. The method of any one of claims 10-30, wherein the determining a treatment regimen comprises treating the subject. 32. The method of any one of claims 10-31, wherein the treatment comprises any one of the treatments provided herein. 33. The method of any one of claims 10-32, wherein the determining a treatment regimen comprises providing information about a treatment to the subject. 34. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject has, is, or will be treated with a therapy, such as any one of the therapies or treatments provided herein, and the method is one to assess the efficacy of the therapy or treatment and/or to determine a treatment regimen. 35. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the sample is a blood, plasma or serum sample. 36. A method of treating a subject having or suspected of having condition, such as an infection, associated with a pro-inflammatory response (e.g., a COVID-19 subject), the 

method comprising administering an effective amount of a cf-DNA inhibitor to the subject or comprising treating the subject such that the amount of cf-DNA is decreased. 37. The method of claim 36, wherein the cf-DNA inhibitor is/comprises cationic nanoparticles, cationic polymers, a DNase, or an inhibitor of TLR9 activation. 38. The method of claim 37, wherein the DNase is DNase I. 39. The method of claim 36, wherein the treating comprises apheresis. 40. The method of claim 36, wherein the treating comprises use of an extracorporeal filter, such as an extracorporeal cytokine adsorber (e.g., CytoSorbÂ®). 41. The method of claim 36, wherein the treating comprises apheresis and/or an extracorporeal filter (e.g., CytoSorbÂ®) as part of an ECMO treatment for any one of the subjects provided herein. 42. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the method further comprises administering an additional therapy or treatment to the subject. 43. The method of claim 42, where the additional therapy or treatment is any one of the therapies or treatments provided herein. 44. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject is any one of the subjects described herein. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein the subject is one with a non-mild pro-inflammatory response. 46. The method of claim 44, wherein the subject is one with a severe pro-inflammatory response. 47. The method of any one of claims 44-46, wherein the subject has or is at risk of having one or more complications of the condition. 48. The method of claim 47, wherein the subject has or is at risk of having pneumonia or ARDS. 49. The method of claim 47, wherein the subject is at increased risk of death. 50. The method of claim 49, wherein the increase is relative to a healthy subject without the condition. 51. The method of claim 49, wherein the increase is relative to a subject with a mild case of the pro-inflammatory response or condition. 52. The method of any one of claims 36-51, wherein the subject is on mechanical support or is in need of mechanical support. 53. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject is one with, including one who was determined to have, an increased level of total cf-DNA. 54. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the method further comprises determining an amount of cf-DNA in one or more samples from the subject. 55. The method of claim 54, wherein the method further comprises comparing the amount of cf-DNA to a threshold cf-DNA value or at least one prior cf-DNA amount. 56. The method of claim 55, wherein the threshold is any one of the thresholds provided herein. 57. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject is one with, including one who was determined to have, a level of cf-DNA greater than any one of the thresholds provided herein. 58. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject is any one of the subjects described herein. 59. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein one or more additional test(s) prior applied to the subject was performed or the method further comprises performing one or more additional test(s) on the subject. 60. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the amount of cf-DNA is determined or obtained using an amplification-based quantification assay. 61. The method of claim 60, wherein the amplification-based quantification assay is quantitative real-time PCR (qRT-PCR) or digital PCR. 62. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject is assessed daily. 63. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the threshold is 10 ng/mL or greater. 64. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the threshold is 25 ng/mL or greater. 65. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the threshold is 50 ng/mL or greater. 66. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the threshold is any one of the thresholds provided herein, including any one of the Examples or Figures.
